features.explanation: This feature activates on word-final suffix morphemes, especially inflectional or derivational endings like Bengali 'ি/িং', Hindi 'ी/ा/ई', and English '-ing' or '-ment'.

features.explanation: This feature fires on digit tokens that begin multi-digit numbers, especially in dates, years, times, percentages, and large quantities.

features.context_pattern: appears inside numbers (years, counts, percentages) or as very short standalone morphemes/characters within running text across languages
features.corpus_consistency: Yes, it consistently activates on single-character tokens across culturax, flores, and xsafety corpora.

features.explanation: This feature activates on the first token of multi-digit numerical expressions such as years, dates, times, percentages, and large quantities.

features.context_pattern: appears in dates, years, times, percentages, monetary amounts, population counts, and other formatted numeric quantities across languages
features.corpus_consistency: yes, it consistently fires on leading digits of numeric expressions in both flores and culturax corpora

features.explanation: This feature fires on explicit negation markers such as 'not', 'n't', and 'no' across languages.

features.context_pattern: Sentences expressing denial, inability, refusal, or absence, typically structured around a negated verb phrase
features.corpus_consistency: Yes—consistently activates on negation across culturax, flores, and xsafety corpora.

features.corpus_consistency: yes, it consistently fires on leading digits of multi-digit numbers in both flores and culturax corpora

features.explanation: This feature activates on short standalone Japanese (and some Chinese) function or morpheme tokens, especially single-kanji stems and interrogative words.
features.token_pattern: single kanji or short kana words such as の, どう, いくつ, 確, 変, 動, 几 that function as grammatical particles, interrogatives, or bound morpheme stems
features.context_pattern: appears across diverse topics but consistently highlights short grammatical or derivational units embedded within otherwise unrelated sentences
features.corpus_consistency: yes, it fires on the same type of short morpheme/function tokens across culturax, flores, and xsafety corpora

features.explanation: This feature activates strongly on single-character tokens, especially standalone digits and other one-character wordpieces across languages.
features.token_pattern: The peak token is a single character (e.g., a lone digit like '1' or '9', or a single Unicode character such as a Bengali, Devanagari, or Chinese character) that forms an individual token.
features.context_pattern: Often appears in numeric contexts (dates, amounts, percentages) or at positions where a single-character word or subword token stands alone within otherwise normal text.
features.corpus_consistency: Yes, it consistently fires on isolated single-character tokens across flores, culturax, and xsafety corpora.

features.explanation: This feature activates on tokens that are continuation pieces rather than word-initial tokens, such as internal digits in numbers and non-initial characters or diacritics within words across scripts.
features.token_pattern: single-character or subword tokens that attach to a preceding token (e.g., internal digits, vowel diacritics, non-space-prefixed pieces)
features.context_pattern: appears inside multi-token words or numbers (years, dates, amounts, times) rather than at the start of a new whitespace-delimited word
features.corpus_consistency: yes—fires on non-initial/continuation tokens across flores, culturax, and xsafety in many languages

features.explanation: This feature activates strongly on the leading digit of multi-digit numerical expressions such as years, dates, percentages, times, and large quantities.
features.token_pattern: a single digit (e.g., 1, 8, 9) that begins a multi-digit number
features.context_pattern: appears in contexts containing years, dates, clock times, percentages, or large numeric quantities across languages
features.corpus_consistency: mostly consistent across flores and culturax with strong focus on numeric expressions, with minor noise cases

features.explanation: This feature fires on the Indic virama/halant diacritic (্ in Bengali, ् in Devanagari) used to suppress the inherent vowel and form consonant clusters.
features.token_pattern: the virama/halant character (্, ्) that appears between consonants in Indic scripts
features.context_pattern: occurs inside consonant clusters within Bengali and Hindi words across diverse topics
features.corpus_consistency: Yes, it consistently fires on the virama character across flores, culturax, and xsafety corpora.
